techniques_used: label-channel ablation (shuffle/random/constant) with matched training set
what_worked: the raw-accuracy contrast is far more legible than PGR here because the weak-strong band is narrow (0.915->0.962), making PGR noisy — report accuracy as the primary axis
matched-input label-channel ablations cleanly isolate per-item signal from format
what_failed: a strong (0.915) weak labeler leaves too little weak->strong headroom, making PGR CIs huge; pick a weaker supervisor next time for a wider band
gotchas: analyze.py must be re-run after the FULL seed sweep lands — the smoke-run artifacts silently persisted here

Semantic Scholar 429'd on both the original and the re-run novelty gate; references.bib had to be hand-built (12 verified entries), so the S2 auto-neighbor path cannot be relied on for the write stage
reusable_pointers: label-channel ablation design generalizes to any W2SG task
novel_vs_kb: The KB's W2SG playbook (scalable-oversight sub-questions 1-2) covers PGR curves and the Burns auxiliary-confidence loss but never a matched format-preserving signal-destruction control; the label-channel ablation isolating per-item weak-label signal from task-format cueing is new, and it yields a clean negative for 'format is enough' that inverts Min et al. 2022's ICL random-label result in the finetuning regime.
